Defines `AppInstallId` type and functions to check if an app ID is system or user-installed.

# Purpose
This C header file defines a type and functions related to application installation identifiers (`AppInstallId`). The `AppInstallId` is a 32-bit integer that uniquely identifies an application while it is installed. System and banked applications have negative identifiers, while applications stored in the AppDB have positive identifiers. The macro `INSTALL_ID_INVALID` is defined as an invalid installation identifier with a value of zero. The file also declares two functions: [`app_install_id_from_system`](<#app_install_id_from_system>), which checks if an `AppInstallId` belongs to a system application, and [`app_install_id_from_app_db`](<#app_install_id_from_app_db>), which checks if an `AppInstallId` belongs to a user-installed application.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdint.h`
- `stdbool.h`
